paper_title: Fossil Coral Analysis and Stable Isotope Data

methods.description: Process for preparing fossil coral samples for analysis, including cutting, screening for diagenetic alteration, and X-ray examination to identify density variations.

steps.description: The fossil corals (n = 15, Table 1) were cut into slabs of < 1 cm thickness along the plane of maximum growth using a conventional rock saw at lowest speed and equipped with a water-cooled diamond blade.
steps.searchable_summary: cut coral samples into slabs using a rock saw

steps.description: All corals were screened for diagenetic alteration using a binocular microscope and SEM.
steps.searchable_summary: screen coral samples for diagenetic alteration using microscope and SEM

steps.description: In order to detect minimal contaminations by secondary calcite, powder samples taken at random were prepared for X-ray diffraction (XRD) and analysed using a Rigaku Miniflex diffractometer with scanning angles of 20 to 60 • 2θ.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze coral samples for secondary calcite using XRD

steps.description: Coral slabs of equal thickness were X-rayed using a digital X-ray cabinet (SHR 50 V) to identify potential zones of diagenetic alteration and to document cyclic density variations.
steps.searchable_summary: X-ray coral slabs to identify diagenetic alteration and density variations

methods.description: Quantitative measurement of coral density using X-ray densitometry and calibration with standards.

steps.description: Quantitative measurements of density were made using X-ray densitometry along transects parallel to the corallites and parallel to the isotope transects.
steps.searchable_summary: measure coral density using X-ray densitometry

steps.description: Calibration of the measurements was tested by measurements of standards for zero density (air) and massive aragonite.

methods.description: Sampling and analysis of stable isotopes from coral samples to determine δ 18 O and δ 13 C values.

steps.description: Sample powders for stable isotope analysis were taken using a microdrill fixed to a manually operated X/Y/Z table.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sample powders for isotope analysis using microdrill

steps.description: Oxygen and carbon stable isotope analyses were carried out at the Institute of Geophysics and Geology, Leipzig University.
steps.searchable_summary: perform stable isotope analysis on coral samples

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the stable isotope data, including correlation and regression analysis.

steps.description: Statistical analyses were performed using the PAST palaeontological statistics software package.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using PAST software

steps.description: Stable isotope data (δ 18 O, δ 13 C) were evaluated using the t test and a linear bivariate model.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate isotope data using t test and linear model
